description: Staff or student profile header displaying name, title, department, role, optional post-nominal letters, and an optional profile picture with modal enlarge functionality.

props.title: Mr
props.department: School of Mathematical and Physical Sciences

stories.name: WithProfilePicture
stories.description: Staff member with profile picture and modal enlarge button

stories.name: WithPostNominalsAndRoles
stories.description: Staff member with post-nominal letters and secondary role

props.title: Professor
props.department: School of Mechanical, Aerospace and Civil Engineering
props.roles: Head of New Interdisciplinary Engineering Programmes

stories.name: NoTitle
stories.description: Person without a title prefix (e.g. PhD student)

props.department: School of Sociological Studies, Politics and International Relations
props.profileImageAlt: Sociological Studies PhD student Nick Morgan

notes: The h1 element contains the title (honorific), first name, and last name as separate text nodes with whitespace — render them space-separated. The title prop is optional as some persons (e.g. PhD students) have no title. The profilepic section is conditionally rendered when profileImageSrc is provided; it contains a .media--image--figure > figure.imgcapt > picture > img, an .enlarge button with a .show-for-sr span for screen readers, and a .profilepic-modal div with a .close button and the modal-sized image. The DeptName atom (.deptname) is always present and is an intrinsic child. The .close button inside .profilepic-modal uses class 'close' (not 'close-button') and contains an <i class='fas fa-times-circle'> icon.
